title: hieracium glaucinum subsp. boudieri
intro: Hieracium glaucinum subsp. boudieri, also known as Boudier's Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family, characterized by its hairy stems and yellow flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Boudier's Hawkweed are said to include reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
